title: Agent identity and key management
description:
Persistent agent identity for clawsig. Today every `wrap` run generates an ephemeral DID — agents have no continuity across runs. Need stable identity for reputation, bounty binding, and fleet management.

Source: clawsig best-practices gap analysis (2026-02-21).
notes: All children closed. Epic complete. 2026-03-19T15:05:23Z.

title: SIG-KEY-001: clawsig init --identity persistent key generation
description:
clawsig init should offer to generate a persistent Ed25519 key pair and store it at a well-known location (~/.clawsig/keys/<project>.jwk.json or .clawsig/identity.jwk.json).

Acceptance criteria:
- clawsig init creates key file if missing
- clawsig wrap auto-discovers key file (env > project > global)
- DID remains stable across runs when key file exists
- Key file permissions set to 600
- Works on macOS + Linux
notes:
Strategy order: 01 (clawsig-v1 critical-path explainer 2026-03-19).
Verified merged via PR #436 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/436). Confirmed ancestor of origin/main at 2026-03-19T11:57:38Z.

title: SIG-KEY-002: DID rotation with continuity proof
description:
When an agent rotates its key (compromise, expiry, fleet reorg), produce a signed rotation proof linking old DID to new DID so verifiers and reputation systems can follow the chain.

Acceptance criteria:
- clawsig rotate-key generates new key + rotation proof
- Rotation proof is a SignedEnvelope with old key signing new DID
- clawverify validates rotation proofs
- Reputation/bounty bindings follow rotation chain
notes: Verified merged via PR #440 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/440). Confirmed ancestor of origin/main at 2026-03-19T11:58:53Z.

title: SIG-KEY-003: Fleet identity registry (multi-agent key management)
description:
For teams running multiple agents, provide a registry mapping agent names/roles to DIDs with key storage, discovery, and revocation.

Acceptance criteria:
- clawsig fleet add <name> generates and registers a key
- clawsig fleet list shows all registered agent DIDs
- clawsig fleet revoke <name> marks key as revoked
- Fleet registry stored at ~/.clawsig/fleet/ or project-level .clawsig/fleet/
- Revoked keys are rejected by clawverify
notes:
Implementation + GPT-5.4 review complete. Branch: feat/clawsig/SIG-KEY-003-fleet-identity. Worktree: /private/tmp/clawsig-swarm/. Awaiting PR push.

title: Product Decoupling (clawsig / clawbounties)
description:
Clean separation of clawsig (L6 proof infrastructure) and clawbounties (agent work marketplace). Two independent products that compose well together.

Clawsig: identity, sentinels, proof bundles, verification, encrypted visibility, compliance, GitHub App
Clawbounties: bounties, claims, submissions, arena, duels, routing, auto-approval

They compose: a bounty submission CAN include a proof bundle for higher trust tier, but doesn't have to.

Roadmap: docs/roadmaps/clawsig-v1/README.md
notes: All children closed. Epic complete. 2026-03-19T15:05:23Z.

description:
Clawbounties is live (prod + staging) but PRD_INDEX.md lists it as 'Archived — dead weight, zero liquidity'. Fix this.

1. Move clawbounties from Archived to Active in docs/PRD_INDEX.md
2. Update docs/prds/clawbounties.md to reflect current state (arena, work loop, marketplace)
3. Update docs/strategy/AGENT_FIRST_ECOSYSTEM.md: skills > MCP, decoupling
4. Add note to AGENT_FIRST_ECOSYSTEM.md: clawbounties stays as marketplace product
5. Keep other archived services archived (escrow, ledger, settle, cuts, etc.)

Acceptance criteria:
- PRD_INDEX.md shows clawbounties as Active
- Clawbounties PRD reflects actual deployed functionality
- Strategy doc updated with skills-over-MCP decision
- No code changes needed

Strategy order: 12 (clawsig-v1 critical-path explainer 2026-03-19).

title: DCP-002: API contract boundary (clawsig vs clawbounties)
description:
Document which API endpoints belong to clawsig (proof infrastructure) vs clawbounties (marketplace).

Write docs/specs/PRODUCT_BOUNDARY_v1.md:

Clawsig domain:
- clawverify.com: verification, schema validation, compliance
- clawsig-ledger: proof metadata, run history
- clawsig-explorer: proof inspector, agent profiles
- clawsig CLI: wrap, verify, init, inspect, compliance
- clawcontrols: Work Policy Contracts
- clawproxy: LLM gateway + receipts

Clawbounties domain:
- clawbounties.com: bounties CRUD, claims, submissions, arena, duels, fleet, routing
- Arena: duel-batch, evaluator, calibration, league, ROI

Shared:
- Identity (DID): used by both, owned by neither
- Proof bundles: produced by clawsig, consumed by clawbounties as optional trust signal

Acceptance criteria:
- Clear boundary documented
- Each API endpoint assigned to one product
- Shared concepts identified with ownership rules
notes:
Strategy order: 13 (clawsig-v1 critical-path explainer 2026-03-19).
Verified merged via PR #442 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/442). Confirmed ancestor of origin/main at 2026-03-19T11:57:39Z.

title: DCP-003: Bounty submissions accept proof bundles as optional trust signal
description:
Currently bounty submissions may require proof bundles. Make proof bundles optional — a trust signal that upgrades the submission tier, not a gate.

- Submission without proof: trust tier = 'test-pass' (validated by exit code only)
- Submission with proof: trust tier = 'gateway' or higher (validated by clawverify)
- Requester can set minimum_proof_tier in bounty spec (default: none)

This ensures clawbounties works independently from clawsig while rewarding proof-attached submissions.

Depends on: monorepo-dcp.2

Acceptance criteria:
- POST /v1/submissions works with or without proof_bundle_uri
- Proof presence upgrades trust tier
- Requester can require minimum tier via bounty spec
- No breaking change to existing submissions
notes:
Implementation + GPT-5.4 review complete. Branch: feat/clawsig/DCP-003-optional-proof-bundles. Worktree: /private/tmp/clawsig-swarm/. Awaiting PR push.

title: DCP-004: Clawsig standalone mode (CI/CD, compliance, no marketplace)
description:
Clawsig must work fully without clawbounties. This is the enterprise CI/CD path:

- clawsig init: creates identity + policy (no marketplace registration)
- clawsig wrap: captures proof bundle (no bounty context needed)
- clawsig verify: validates bundle offline
- clawsig inspect: shows bundle contents
- GitHub App: verifies PRs against .clawsig/policy.json (no clawbounties dependency)

Acceptance criteria:
- clawsig init works without --marketplace flag
- clawsig wrap works without active bounty
- All verification is offline-capable
- GitHub App only depends on clawverify, not clawbounties
- README/docs clearly describe the standalone path
notes:
Implementation + GPT-5.4 review complete. Branch: feat/clawsig/DCP-004-standalone-mode. Worktree: /private/tmp/clawsig-swarm/. Awaiting PR push.

title: Encrypted Proof Visibility (EPV)
description:
Access-controlled proof bundles. Public hash layer for verification + encrypted plaintext layer for authorized viewers. Ties agent DIDs to human GitHub identity. Visibility modes: public (default), owner, requester, auditor.

Key insight: current bundles have hashed fields (command_hash_b64u instead of command) making them unreadable. EPV adds an encrypted layer with full plaintext that only authorized viewers can decrypt. The hash layer remains for public verification.

Spec: docs/specs/clawsig-protocol/ENCRYPTED_PROOF_VISIBILITY_v1.md
Roadmap: docs/roadmaps/clawsig-v1/README.md
notes: All children closed. Epic complete. 2026-03-19T15:05:23Z.

title: EPV-001: proof_bundle.v2 schema with encrypted payload + viewer keys
description:
Define proof_bundle.v2.json schema extension:

- public_layer: hash-only receipts, signatures, DID, timestamps (current behavior)
- encrypted_payload: AES-256-GCM encrypted blob containing plaintext commands, file paths, tool call args, LLM request/response summaries
- viewer_keys[]: array of { did, wrapped_key } — each viewer's DID mapped to the AES key encrypted with their public key
- visibility: enum (public | owner | requester | auditor)

Acceptance criteria:
- Schema defined at packages/schema/poh/proof_bundle.v2.json
- Backward compatible: v1 bundles remain valid
- Encrypted payload is optional (public visibility = no encrypted payload)
- Each viewer key slot wraps the same AES-256-GCM key with the viewer's Ed25519 public key (X25519 ECDH)
- clawverify validates v2 bundles (public layer only — does not need decryption key)
- Spec written: docs/specs/clawsig-protocol/ENCRYPTED_PROOF_VISIBILITY_v1.md
notes:
Strategy order: 03 (clawsig-v1 critical-path explainer 2026-03-19).
Verified merged via PR #437 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/437). Confirmed ancestor of origin/main at 2026-03-19T11:57:38Z.

title: EPV-002: clawsig wrap --visibility modes
description:
Add --visibility flag to clawsig wrap:

- --visibility public (default): hash-only bundle, no encrypted payload (current behavior)
- --visibility owner: encrypted payload with viewer key for the operator's persistent DID
- --visibility requester: encrypted payload with viewer keys for operator DID + requester DID (from active bounty)
- --visibility auditor: selective disclosure — reveal tool names + file paths but not content

Implementation:
- wrap.ts generates AES-256-GCM key per run
- Encrypts plaintext receipt data (commands, paths, args) into encrypted_payload
- Wraps AES key with each viewer's public key via X25519 ECDH
- Writes proof_bundle.v2.json with both layers

Depends on: monorepo-20a.1 (persistent identity for operator DID), monorepo-epv.1 (schema)

Acceptance criteria:
- clawsig wrap --visibility owner produces v2 bundle with encrypted payload
- clawsig wrap (no flag) produces v1-compatible bundle (no encrypted payload)
- Encrypted payload contains plaintext: commands, file paths, tool call names + args, network hosts
- Viewer keys correctly wrap the AES key for each authorized viewer
- Bundle size overhead < 20% vs public mode
notes:
Strategy order: 04 (clawsig-v1 critical-path explainer 2026-03-19).
Verified merged via PR #444 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/444). Confirmed ancestor of origin/main at 2026-03-19T11:57:38Z.

title: EPV-003: clawsig inspect --decrypt
description:
New subcommand: clawsig inspect <bundle.json> [--decrypt]

Without --decrypt: shows public layer (hashes, signatures, receipt count, DID, timestamps, tier)
With --decrypt: loads operator's persistent key, unwraps AES key from viewer_keys, decrypts encrypted_payload, shows full plaintext forensics

Output format:
- Default: human-readable summary (tool calls with args, file paths, commands, network hosts)
- --json: machine-readable decrypted payload

Depends on: monorepo-epv.2

Acceptance criteria:
- clawsig inspect shows public layer for any v1 or v2 bundle
- clawsig inspect --decrypt decrypts v2 bundles if operator's DID is in viewer_keys
- Fails cleanly if DID not in viewer_keys ("You are not authorized to view this bundle's plaintext")
- Fails cleanly on v1 bundles ("This bundle has no encrypted payload")
- --json outputs structured decrypted data
notes:
Strategy order: 05 (clawsig-v1 critical-path explainer 2026-03-19).
Verified merged via PR #446 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/446). Confirmed ancestor of origin/main at 2026-03-19T11:57:38Z.

title: EPV-004: GitHub-DID binding attestation
description:
Link a human's GitHub identity to their agent DIDs.

clawsig identity link-github --github <username>
- Opens browser for GitHub OAuth
- Creates signed attestation: 'GitHub user X controls DID Y'
- Stored locally at ~/.clawsig/github-binding.json
- Optionally published to clawsig ledger

One human can have many DIDs (one per session, agent, machine). All linked back to the GitHub identity.

Used by:
- Explorer: authenticate with GitHub, see all your agent runs
- Bounty requester: verify the agent's owner identity
- Encrypted visibility: GitHub user gets a viewer key slot automatically

Depends on: monorepo-20a.1

Acceptance criteria:
- OAuth flow authenticates with GitHub
- Binding attestation is signed with persistent DID
- Multiple DIDs can bind to same GitHub user
- clawsig identity show lists GitHub binding + all linked DIDs
notes:
Implementation + GPT-5.4 review complete. Branch: feat/clawsig/EPV-004-github-did-binding. Worktree: /private/tmp/clawsig-swarm/. Awaiting PR push.

Add clawsig work subcommands so agents can discover, claim, execute, and submit bounties from the CLI. This is the git+gh equivalent for the agent economy.

title: AWL-001: clawsig init generates persistent identity + registers with marketplace
description:
Upgrade clawsig init to:
1. Generate persistent Ed25519 key at ~/.clawsig/identity.jwk.json (or .clawsig/identity.jwk.json)
2. Derive DID from key
3. Prompt for clawbounties marketplace URL (default: clawbounties.com)
4. POST /v1/workers/register with the DID
5. Store marketplace config in .clawsig/marketplace.json
6. Print onboarding summary: DID, marketplace URL, next steps

Depends on: monorepo-20a.1 (persistent key generation)

Acceptance criteria:
- clawsig init creates identity key if missing
- clawsig init registers with marketplace
- Subsequent clawsig commands auto-discover identity + marketplace config
- Works without marketplace (offline mode: key only, no registration)

description:

title: Agent Interface Skill
description:
SKILL.md + CLI --json as the canonical agent interface. Agents read the skill file, learn the CLI commands, execute via bash. No MCP server needed.

This replaces the MCP server recommendation from the deep-think synthesis. Skills are the proven pattern (Gmail, Calendar, Wrangler all work this way).

Skill location: ~/.pi/agent/skills/clawsig/SKILL.md
Roadmap: docs/roadmaps/clawsig-v1/README.md

title: SKL-001: --json flag on all CLI commands
description:
Every clawsig/clawverify CLI command must support --json for machine-parseable output. Stolen from GitHub's gh CLI pattern.

Commands to update:
- clawsig wrap: emit JSON summary on exit (status, cost, tools, coverage, bundle path)
- clawsig init: JSON output of identity + config created
- clawverify verify: JSON verification result
- clawverify compliance: already JSON
- clawverify explain: JSON reason code details
- clawsig inspect: JSON public layer (and --decrypt JSON for decrypted)
- clawsig work *: all JSON by default (agent-facing)

Acceptance criteria:
- All commands accept --json flag
- JSON output is strict, parseable, no ANSI escape codes
- Default (no --json) remains human-readable with colors
- Error responses are also JSON when --json is set

Write ~/.pi/agent/skills/clawsig/SKILL.md — the canonical agent interface for the Clawsig protocol.

The skill teaches agents:
1. How to set up identity (clawsig init)
2. How to discover and claim bounties (clawsig work list/claim)
3. How to execute with proof capture (clawsig wrap)
4. How to submit work (clawsig work submit)
5. How to check status (clawsig work status)

All commands shown with --json flag. Includes example JSON outputs.

Pattern: follow existing skills (brave-search, wrangler, gmcli) for structure.

Depends on: monorepo-skl.1 (--json), monorepo-20a.1 (identity)

Acceptance criteria:

title: SKL-003: Quiet clawsig wrap default output
description:
Default clawsig wrap output is currently a firehose of sentinel diagnostic logs. Replace with clean spinner + summary box on exit.

Before:
  FS Sentinel: ACTIVE (14,102 raw syscalls logged)
  Net Sentinel: 150 TCP connections intercepted
  Local proxy listening on 127.0.0.1:38291
  ...1681 lines of noise...

After:
  ⠧ Securing agent execution environment...
  ✔ Agent exited with code 0
    Coverage : Gateway + Tools
    Cost     : $0.04 (14 LLM calls)
    Network  : 2 external connections
    Bundle   : .clawsig/proof_bundle.v2.json (26 KB)

Add --verbose flag for current behavior (useful for debugging sentinel issues).

Also: filter null-field receipts at emission time. If command === null AND target === null, don't include in bundle.

Acceptance criteria:
- Default output is <10 lines
- Summary box shows coverage, cost, network, bundle path
- --verbose restores current behavior
- Null-field execution receipts filtered out
- Bundle size reduced by >50% for typical pi/claude-code runs
notes:
Strategy order: 11 (clawsig-v1 critical-path explainer 2026-03-19).
Verified merged via PR #438 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/438). Confirmed ancestor of origin/main at 2026-03-19T11:57:39Z.

title: SKL-004: run_summary.json — agent-readable bundle summary
description:
Generate a lightweight run_summary.json alongside proof_bundle.json. This is the 150-token distillation agents read instead of the full 50KB+ bundle.

Format:
{
  "status": "PASS",
  "tier": "gateway",
  "cost_usd": 0.05,
  "tools_used": ["Read", "Edit", "Bash"],
  "files_modified": ["src/index.ts"],
  "policy_violations": 0,
  "network_connections": 2,
  "bundle_path": ".clawsig/proof_bundle.v2.json",
  "bundle_size_bytes": 26000,
  "agent_did": "did:key:z6Mk...",
  "run_id": "run_abc123"
}

Depends on: monorepo-skl.1 (--json for consistency)

Acceptance criteria:
- Written to .clawsig/run_summary.json after every wrap
- < 200 tokens when serialized
- Contains actionable info: status, tier, tools, files, violations
- Agents can read this instead of the full bundle
notes:
Implementation + GPT-5.4 review complete. Branch: feat/clawsig/SKL-004-run-summary-json. Worktree: /private/tmp/clawsig-swarm/. Awaiting PR push.
